description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a prominent pitched red tile roof featuring multiple intersecting gables and dormers. The exterior walls are primarily constructed of brick, with exposed wooden beams visible under the eaves and in the gable ends, adding a rustic touch. Large windows are distributed asymmetrically around the structure, providing ample natural light to the interior. The house includes a covered balcony on the upper floor and a spacious porch area beneath, supported by sturdy columns. The foundation appears to be made of concrete, elevating the structure slightly above ground level. The overall design blends traditional and craftsman architectural elements, making it suitable for suburban or semi-urban neighborhoods.
description: A two-story brick house with a complex red gable roof, exposed wooden beams, large windows, and a covered balcony and porch.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the main porch, balcony, and exposed wooden beams under the gable.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the intersecting gables, additional roof sections, and more window placements.
description_view_2: Top-down view emphasizing the expansive red tiled roof and dormer windows.
description_view_3: Rear-right view displaying the balcony, brickwork, and secondary gable features.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a covered porch with supporting columns and a balcony above, accented by exposed wooden beams.
